projectDescription: A fast, open-source document parser with spatial text extraction, OCR, and multi-format support that runs entirely locally.
keyFeatures: Spatial text extraction with precise bounding boxes using PDF.js
Built-in Tesseract.js OCR with zero setup required
Pluggable HTTP OCR server support (EasyOCR, PaddleOCR, custom)
Multi-format document input via LibreOffice and ImageMagick conversion
High-quality page screenshot generation for LLM agents
Standalone binary with no cloud dependencies
publicApiSurface: LiteParse (class) — main parser with parse() and screenshot() methods
LiteParseConfig — configuration interface with OCR, DPI, output format options
ParseResult — result type with pages, text, and optional JSON output
ParsedPage — per-page data with textItems, bounding boxes, and dimensions
TextItem — individual extracted text element with position and font metadata
ScreenshotResult — screenshot result with imageBuffer and dimensions
searchItems(items, options) — utility to search text items by phrase
BoundingBox — deprecated bounding box type (x1, y1, x2, y2)
lit parse — CLI command for parsing documents
lit batch-parse — CLI command for batch directory parsing
lit screenshot — CLI command for generating page screenshots
POST /ocr — OCR server HTTP endpoint specification
